free_text: Inelastic scattering of fast neutrons from U-238
3MeV Van de Graaff with terminal pulsing, pulse
length was 1 to 2 ns and repetition frequency 3 MHz,
The scatterer was pure natural uranium in the
form of a hollow cylinder, 25mm high,inside and outside
Sample at 8cm from n-source, flight path varied between
Time of flight of scattered neutrons
Standard sample had same outside dimen-
sions as uranium sample but 2mm thick walls. Values
used were those of langsdorf et al, ANL-5567(1961)rev.
Poor agreement with previous work was found for the
45 keV level, this may partly be due to effect of
elastic scattering not having been evaluated correctly.
Data received from Reitmann, 1970/5/6, later pu
Corrections were made for flux attenuation in U and C,
multiple scattering in U, long counter efficiency and
for second neutron group in Li-Be reaction. Resultant
correction never exceded 5% on raw data.
Errors given are due to statistics plus a 5%
allowance for uncertainties in detector efficiency.
No contribution for uncertainty in the standard
Energy resolution was better than 10 keV
